heading: Transparency in extractive industries resource payments
label: 17374 Transparency in extractive industries resource payments
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2

children.text: ensure greater United States energy security by combating corruption in the governments of foreign countries that receive revenues from the sale of their natural resources; and
enhance the development of democracy and increase political and economic stability in such resource rich foreign countries.
to increase energy security by promoting anti-corruption initiatives in oil and natural gas rich countries; and

to promote global energy security through promotion of programs such as the Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative (EITI) that seek to instill transparency and accountability into extractive industries resource payments.
encouraging further participation in the EITI by eligible countries and companies; and
promoting the efficacy of the EITI program by ensuring a robust and candid review mechanism.

children.heading: Report required
children.label: 1 Report required
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary of Energy, shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on progress made in promoting transparency in extractive industries resource payments.December 19, 2007

children.heading: Matters to be included
children.label: 2 Matters to be included
children.text: The report required by paragraph (1) shall include a detailed description of United States participation in the EITI, bilateral and multilateral diplomatic efforts to further participation in the EITI, and other United States initiatives to strengthen energy security, deter energy kleptocracy, and promote transparency in the extractive industries.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated $3,000,000 for the purposes of United States contributions to the Multi-Donor Trust Fund of the EITI.
